summary: 本轮检索到 1 项新的高相关工作：`VibeAct`，它把振动信号用于接触丰富的反应式灵巧操作控制，主题与灵巧机器人手直接相关，且发布时间晚于上一轮成功运行，因此应作为新增条目收录。整体来看，本轮没有发现大量新文献，但这项工作在触觉感知与灵巧操作闭环控制之间建立了清晰连接，值得继续跟进其后续代码与实机结果。
summary_en: This run found 1 newly relevant item: `VibeAct`, which uses vibration signals for contact-rich reactive robot dexterity. It is directly aligned with dexterous robot hands and was published after the previous successful run, so it should be added as a new item. No broad wave of new papers appeared in this run, but this work is notable because it connects tactile sensing to closed-loop dexterous manipulation in a concrete way.

items.title: VibeAct: Vibration to Actions for Contact-Rich Reactive Robot Dexterity
items.authors_or_org: arXiv 论文；公开页面未在本轮检索中完整展开作者列表
items.relevance: 直接围绕灵巧机器人操作中的接触响应控制展开，振动感知是灵巧手闭环控制的核心输入。
items.relevance_en: It is directly about contact-reactive control for dexterous robot manipulation, with vibration sensing as a core input for hand control.
items.novelty_signal: 2026-06-25 新近公开，主题聚焦接触丰富的反应式灵巧操作，且明显区别于通用抓取或非手部操作。
items.novelty_signal_en: Freshly public on 2026-06-25, focused on contact-rich reactive dexterity and clearly distinct from generic grasping or non-hand manipulation.
items.analysis_zh: 这项工作的主要工作是提出 `VibeAct`，把振动信号直接用于接触丰富场景下的反应式灵巧控制，核心目标是让机器人手在与物体发生接触、滑移或扰动时能更快地产生合适动作。它解决的是灵巧操作中“接触状态变化快、仅靠视觉或低频状态反馈难以及时调整”的问题，因此方法上强调从振动到动作的映射，并把触觉信息纳入闭环决策。公开信息表明它聚焦灵巧机器人手这一中心对象，但本轮检索到的摘要细节不足，尚不能完整确认具体模型结构、实验平台与基线设置。就现有信息看，这项工作的重要性在于把振动这一高频接触线索与灵巧手控制直接关联起来，可能对滑移检测、抓握稳定性和快速重抓具有价值；限制是公开材料尚不足以支撑对实验规模、定量提升和泛化能力的充分判断。
items.analysis_en: The main contribution is `VibeAct`, a method that turns vibration signals into actions for contact-rich reactive robot dexterity. It addresses a core dexterous-manipulation problem: contact conditions can change too quickly for vision-only or low-rate state feedback to react in time, so the hand needs a faster signal path for recovery and control. Methodologically, the public information indicates a mapping from vibration to control actions, with tactile information treated as part of the closed-loop policy for the robot hand. The available abstract-level evidence makes the hand-centric focus clear, but it does not fully disclose the model architecture, the exact hardware setup, or the benchmark baselines used. Overall, the work is relevant because it ties a high-frequency tactile cue to dexterous hand control in a concrete way and could be useful for slip recovery and grasp stabilization; the limitation is that the public materials available in this run are insufficient to assess experimental scale, quantitative gains, and generalization strength.

caveats: 本轮优先检索 arXiv 与原始项目页，严格按灵巧机器人手、灵巧操作、触觉感知和反应式控制筛选。
公开材料仅足以确认该条目的相关性与发布时间，实验细节、基线和定量结果尚未在本轮可见页面中完整展开，因此分析保持保守。
本轮仅新增 1 项高相关条目，已同步更新日报、latest、daily、reports 与 seen 状态文件。
